body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

`token-accounting` stresses per-turn token/cost accounting across MULTIPLE turns: after turn 1 `TotalTokens > 0`; after each later turn `TotalTokens` is non-decreasing; and when a turn runs against prompt cache, cache-read tokens are bucketed separately from fresh input. The primitive is token reporting (input/output/cache read+write in a transcript field the parser can extract) and it requires `headless_mode`.

**agent_supports: yes.** Codex CLI 0.130.0 emits a `token_count` event_msg on every API turn whose `payload.info` carries two usage blocks: `last_token_usage` (per-turn snapshot) and `total_token_usage` (cumulative running total across all turns in the session). OpenAI's usage shape nests cache hits under `input_tokens_details.cached_tokens`. Multiple turns in one rollout produce a monotonically advancing `total_token_usage`, which is exactly the accumulation the scenario asserts.

**irrlicht_observes: yes.** The codex adapter watches rollout transcripts via `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir(), Parser: JSONLineParser{...}}` (agent.go). The parser (parser.go) is stateful: it keeps a `cursor` of the last committed cumulative `total_token_usage` and emits a `PerTurnContribution{Usage: UsageBreakdown{Input, Output, CacheRead}}` for the delta (`current − cursor`) on each turn boundary, advancing the cursor only when the delta is positive (`extractCodexMetadata` + the delta block in `ParseLine`). `extractOpenAIUsageBreakdown` routes `input_tokens_details.cached_tokens` to `CacheRead` and deducts it from `Input` to avoid double-counting (OpenAI already includes cached tokens in `input_tokens`). `CumulativeTokens`/`Tokens` are surfaced for `SessionMetrics.TotalTokens`, which therefore accumulates non-decreasingly across the three turns. The parser also persists the cumulative cursor via `GetParserLedger`/`SetParserLedger` so deltas stay correct across restarts. Parser unit test `TestParser_MultiTurnTokenCount_UsesPerTurnSnapshot` covers exactly the multi-turn per-turn-snapshot accounting; the coverage matrix already records codex/token-accounting as yes/yes ("cumulative cursor → delta math; cached_tokens deducted to avoid double-count"), and this assessment ratifies it.

The state arc is unremarkable (three `ready→working→ready` cycles on one rollout session); the token-accounting evidence lives in `events.jsonl` payloads (`PerTurnContribution` per turn boundary + accumulating `TotalTokens`), not in the phase-DSL state assertions. The phase DSL has no metric form, so the spec asserts the three-turn state arc (the recording's structural skeleton) and the token counts are verified via the recipe's plain-English `verify` bullets + the parser unit test.

No carve-outs apply to observability. One environmental caveat: the daemon has logged "no pricing for model gpt-5.3-codex — cost will be 0 until LiteLLM cache is refreshed" for some codex models, so `estimated_cost_usd` may read $0 even when token counts are real. Token accounting (the counts/deltas) is the user-observable signal this scenario is about and is unaffected; $0 cost on an un-priced model is a pricing-cache limitation, not a token-accounting gap.
caveats: Cost may read $0 for codex models the LiteLLM pricing cache doesn't yet know (logged for gpt-5.3-codex); token counts/deltas are still real and observable — treat $0 cost as a pricing-cache limitation, not a token-accounting gap.

sources.note: stateful cursor → per-turn delta PerTurnContribution; extractCodexMetadata reads last_token_usage + total_token_usage; extractOpenAIUsageBreakdown routes input_tokens_details.cached_tokens to CacheRead and deducts from Input; GetParserLedger/SetParserLedger persist cursor
FilesUnderRoot + JSONLineParser transport over the rollout sessions dir
matrix records codex/token-accounting yes/yes — cumulative cursor → delta math; cached_tokens deducted
token-accounting: TotalTokens>0 after T1, non-decreasing after T2, CacheReadTokens>0 with fresh input=0 on a fully-cached turn
